title: ‘The Flash’ Is Here! Is It the "Greatest Superhero Movie Ever"?
format: Sean Fennessey and Amanda Dobbins bring in Van Lathan to argue over whether The Flash lives up to James Gunn calling it one of the greatest superhero movies ever made.

segments.heading: The Flash
segments.note: with Van Lathan of Higher Learning and The Midnight Boys — spoilers throughout

films.title: The Flash
films.note: Van liked it, breaking from the other two; he says the movie's total narrative freedom (no idea who survives the coming Gunn-era reboot) gave it real stakes, but even he admits Ezra Miller's performance is grating enough to sink the two scenes that needed to land emotionally. Sean and Amanda call the first and third acts a mess and single out the CGI multiverse sequence as a disaster; Sean says it's 'like a bear ate a can of paint and then threw up.' All three land on Michael Keaton's older Batman and Sasha Calle's Supergirl as the highlights, and on the digitally revived Christopher Reeve cameo as shameless but effective.

segments.heading: Multiverse fatigue and the DCU's stitched-together problem

films.title: Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse
films.note: Seen back-to-back with The Flash on a double bill that did The Flash no favors. Amanda liked it less than the first film once the multiversal stakes got, in her word, 'muddied' in the last fifteen minutes, though both found it visually stunning.

films.title: Spider-Man: No Way Home
films.note: Sean's comparison point for The Flash's parade of legacy cameos, the same trick Sony already ran minus the emotional stakes.

films.title: Everything Everywhere All at Once
films.note: Named as part of the same multiversal glut The Flash is arriving into.

films.title: X-Men: Days of Future Past
films.note: Van's gold standard for a speedster set piece; Evan Peters' Quicksilver rescue is the two-minute sequence he says basically made a Flash movie unnecessary.

films.title: X-Men: Apocalypse
films.note: Quicksilver's second, higher-stakes rescue, which Van says The Flash's baby-filled opening is trying to top.

films.title: Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 3
films.note: Van's comparison for The Flash's cheap emotional manipulation: the otter with the little clamped hands got him the same way the Christopher Reeve cameo does.

films.title: Man of Steel
films.note: Where Michael Shannon's General Zod, restored here as the villain, first showed up.

films.title: Batman v Superman: Dawn of Justice
films.note: Introduces Jeffrey Dean Morgan's Thomas Wayne; everyone in the room says they saw his Flashpoint-universe Batman turn coming the moment the nightmare-sequence time jump happened.

films.title: The Batman
films.note: Prompts a detour into how Robert Pattinson's explicitly separate-universe Batman changed the way Van watches recastings: purposeful multiverse management instead of an accident of behind-the-scenes rebooting.

films.title: Shazam! Fury of the Gods
films.note: Van couldn't finish it 'even for employment'; both needle the DCEU's habit of shoehorning in a pointless Wonder Woman cameo, which this one also does.

films.title: Prey
films.note: Van's example of legacy-franchise storytelling actually working: a Predator movie he connected to because it foregrounded character over the alien.

films.title: Avengers: Endgame
films.note: Van's thesis for why the multiverse trick doesn't work here: Endgame's ensemble battle earns its weight because ten years of movies built those relationships brick by brick in one continuous universe, not stitched together after the fact like The Flash.

films.title: Captain America: The Winter Soldier
films.note: Sean's example of the grounded, single-universe scale The Flash abandons by inflating the stakes to a multiverse.

films.title: Spider-Man 3
films.note: Van's shorthand for why Sony rebooted the franchise in the first place: 'they fucked up Spider-Man 3.'

films.title: The Amazing Spider-Man 2
films.note: The other reason Sony started over: 'the Amazing Spider-Man 2 wasn't any good, and we signed a deal with Marvel.'

segments.heading: A rough May before The Flash

films.title: The Little Mermaid
films.note: Amanda's 'struggles' with it, and Sean's on-air meltdown reviewing it, get blamed for a genuinely dark stretch of programming leading into this episode.

films.title: Aladdin
films.note: Amanda didn't like it, part of a pattern with Disney's live-action remakes she calls the most pointless endeavor in movies outside of making money.

films.title: The Lion King
films.note: Called a 'goddamn abomination,' not a knock on the cast, just the format.

Ads: FIFA World Cup Launch Edition (Netflix ad)

Batman v Superman: Dawn of Justice.title: Batman v Superman: Dawn of Justice
